hnote_item_addressed: Item 4 — 'How to Evaluate' 5-question Overview preface (linked questions to demonstrating sections)
scope_item: Promote existing eg-evaluate ("How to Evaluate Any Scientific Claim") into a 5-question preface block on the Overview tab. Each question is a link to the section in the review that demonstrates the failure mode the question detects.

The questions are already the canonical phrasing operator-authored when eg-evaluate was created; reusing verbatim per HNOTE 'no derived content' / 'no invented conditions' constraint.

All five targets are stable today and unaffected by EXP-253/254/255/256 integration.

target: build-scripts/generate-html.js — insert a new <section class="evaluate-preface"> on the Overview tab IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the existing <section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section"> block (current line 1388), and add a corresponding CSS rule block AFTER the existing `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}` rule (current line 537). The preface sits BELOW the EXP-255 thesis-hero (whether or not EXP-255 has integrated) and ABOVE the accuracy-math-section. It does NOT depend on EXP-190 (parked) and does not duplicate the EXP-256 falsifiability-module — the falsifiability-module answers "what would change OUR verdict" (specific to this review), while the 5-question preface answers "how should ANY claim be evaluated" (universal methodology).

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique — `grep -c` on the literal string returns exactly 1; the line currently sits at ~1388 in the Overview tab and is unaffected by EXP-255 (which edits the .scorecard sc-hero block IMMEDIATELY ABOVE this anchor) and EXP-256 (which inserts a falsifiability-module IMMEDIATELY AFTER the generateVerdictBarChart() call, several blocks above this anchor). Order-of-integration robustness verified: regardless of whether EXP-255 and/or EXP-256 land first, this anchor remains unique and stable.
patch_segments.insert_text:
Five questions to ask of any scientific claim

From this review or any other — each question links to where this review tests it. Full preface →

  1. 1 Does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives? Standard Model Explains (3.4)
  2. 2 Can the prediction be derived from the model’s own parameters? Self-Contradicted (3.3)
  3. 3 Is the cited data accurately represented? Refuted by Data (3.2)
  4. 4 Is the claim falsifiable? Misleading & Unfalsifiable (3.6)
  5. 5 Are the same data being counted multiple times? De-Duplication Analysis (6.6b)
patch_segments.collision_note: No collision with EXP-255 or EXP-256. EXP-255 modifies the sc-hero block (lines ~1366-1383 in current file) which sits BETWEEN the verdict bar chart and this anchor. EXP-256 inserts after the verdict bar chart anchor (line 1364), several lines ABOVE this anchor. EXP-257's insert lands between the (post-EXP-255) thesis-hero and the accuracy-math-section, with no overlap. Visual flow after all three integrate: title-block → verdict-bar → falsifiability-module (EXP-256) → thesis-hero (EXP-255) → evaluate-preface (EXP-257) → accuracy-math-section.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique — `grep -c` on the literal string returns exactly 1; the rule currently sits at ~line 537. EXP-255 inserts CSS between `.sc-domains{...}` and `.verdict-bars{margin:...}` (one line ABOVE this anchor); EXP-256 inserts CSS after the .accuracy-math-section @media rule (several lines ABOVE this anchor). Both EXP-255 and EXP-256 leave the .verdict-bars .vb-caption rule untouched. Order-of-integration robustness verified.
patch_segments.design_token_audit: All colors routed through existing CSS variables — no hardcoded hex values. border-left uses --ink-3 (neutral, deliberately distinct from the EXP-256 falsifiability-module's --accent left rail to signal 'methodology guide' rather than 'verdict-related criteria'). ep-heading uses --ink-3 and matches the EXP-256 .fm-heading style for visual consistency between the two structured Overview blocks. ep-num and ep-where use --ink-3; ep-q uses --heading with --accent on hover. Background --card-bg matches both .falsifiability-module and existing legend cards. Dark mode inherits via :root variable swap at line ~497.

target_section_eg_evaluate.tldr_quote_verbatim: Five questions to ask of any claim — from the dome model or from us: Does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives? Can it be derived from the model's own parameters? Is the data accurately represented? Is the claim falsifiable? Are the same data counted multiple times?

five_question_link_targets.question: Does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives?
five_question_link_targets.rationale: Std Model Explains is the canonical verdict bucket for non-discriminating claims — exactly the failure mode this question detects. The bucket currently contains 18 of 71 claims (25.4%). Each WIN there is a case where the dome and the globe predict the same value; the dome's relabeling adds no discriminating signal.

five_question_link_targets.question: Can the prediction be derived from the model's own parameters?
five_question_link_targets.rationale: Self-Contradicted is the verdict bucket for WINs whose claimed value contradicts the dome's own published equations when those equations are applied honestly — the inverse failure of question 2. Currently 14 of 71 claims (19.7%). Including 2.1 (Schumann), 2.2 (tidal), 2.3 (gravity gradient): each is a case where dome geometry produces the wrong answer for the dome's own claim.

five_question_link_targets.question: Is the cited data accurately represented?
five_question_link_targets.rationale: Refuted by Data is the bucket for claims where the cited dataset, when actually consulted, does not support the dome's quantitative claim. Currently 9 of 71 claims (12.7%). Direct mapping: each WIN there exemplifies a failure of question 3.

five_question_link_targets.question: Is the claim falsifiable?
five_question_link_targets.rationale: The combined Misleading/Unfalsifiable bucket includes the 4 unfalsifiable verdicts (5.6% of 71) plus 22 Misleading. Aetheric refraction with no specified index function, ad-hoc 'optical illusion' escape hatches, and other unfalsifiable mechanisms are the canonical failure mode of question 4. The bucket header explicitly names 'Unfalsifiable' alongside Misleading.

five_question_link_targets.question: Are the same data being counted multiple times?
five_question_link_targets.rationale: Section 6.6b explicitly performs a conservative de-duplication clustering and reports the count drops from 69 to ~40 independent claims when shared data sources / derivation chains are folded. The TLDR opens with 'The dome counts 69 confirmed predictions, but many are multiple descriptions of the same observation' — direct mapping to question 5. Tidal cluster (2.2) and the new-content-breakdown table also discuss count inflation but 6.6b is the structural treatment.

placement_decision.chose: BELOW the thesis-hero (before accuracy-math-section), at body weight
placement_decision.operator_options_offered: ABOVE the thesis sentence as eyebrow / preface block, OR below the thesis at body weight
placement_decision.rationale: Above the thesis is already congested after EXP-256 integration: verdict bar chart followed by falsifiability-module (4-row structured block) — adding a second 5-row structured block ABOVE the thesis would create a stacking wall before the reader reaches the punchline. Placing the preface BELOW the thesis at body weight preserves the thesis-hero as the visual climax of the first viewport, then offers the methodology-rest-of-the-document ('here's how we got there, and here's how you can re-evaluate') in the natural reading order. The 'preface' label still applies because the questions preface the rest of the review (the wins, kill-shots, predictions) — not the thesis itself.
placement_decision.visual_hierarchy_check: Three structured Overview blocks now stack: (a) EXP-256 falsifiability-module — left rail --accent, framed as 'verdict criteria'; (b) EXP-255 thesis-hero — display-scale serif, the headline; (c) EXP-257 evaluate-preface — left rail --ink-3, framed as 'methodology' (not verdict-related). Visual differentiation between (a) and (c) preserved by left-rail color and ep-q serif vs fm-condition sans treatment. The thesis-hero (b) sits between them with maximum visual weight, fulfilling the climax role.
placement_decision.above_the_fold_estimate: On a 1440x900 desktop viewport, post-EXP-255 measurements estimated the thesis-hero composition at ~666px below the title-block. Adding the EXP-256 falsifiability-module (estimated ~280px) keeps the thesis-hero above the fold by roughly 200-250px on a typical desktop. Adding the EXP-257 evaluate-preface BELOW the thesis-hero pushes additional content below the fold — but that is the correct hierarchy: the thesis is what the reader sees first, the methodology is what they scroll to.

hero_composition_check.thesis_remains_visual_climax: yes — evaluate-preface is body-weight (font-size:0.95rem serif), no display-scale type, neutral --ink-3 left rail
hero_composition_check.no_competing_color_with_eclipse_badge: yes — preface uses --ink-3 left rail, eclipse badge uses --accent (deliberately distinct)
hero_composition_check.no_competing_with_falsifiability_module: yes — preface uses different visual vocabulary (numbered list, ep-q serif Q&A, ep-where right-aligned label) vs falsifiability-module's grid layout (condition / desc / status). Reader can distinguish the two at a glance.
hero_composition_check.progressive_disclosure_compliant: no — this is a static Overview block, not a section with a TLDR (matches the pattern set by EXP-256 falsifiability-module which is also a static Overview block, not a <details> wrapper). Operator brief explicitly framed Item 4 as a 'preface block' and Item 3 as a 'persistent module' — both intended to be always-visible on the Overview, not collapsed.

load_bearing_content_audit.audit_method: Compared the original eg-evaluate section content against the EXP-257 preface to verify nothing load-bearing is omitted (preface is additive — eg-evaluate stays in the Evaluation Guide tab unchanged).
load_bearing_content_audit.eg_evaluate_remains_canonical: yes — the full eg-evaluate section (with the 'medicine analogy' that operator brief explicitly praises) stays in tab 'evaluate' at id='eg-evaluate'. The preface caption explicitly links to it ('Full preface →'). Readers who want the long-form treatment one click away.
load_bearing_content_audit.five_questions_preserved_verbatim: yes — the 5 questions are quoted verbatim from the existing eg-evaluate body and TLDR. Punctuation: question 2 uses 'model's' with rsquo entity (the existing eg-evaluate uses straight quote); question 4 'Misleading & Unfalsifiable' uses ampersand entity (the section heading uses 'and'). These are surface-level typography choices, not content changes.
load_bearing_content_audit.no_invented_questions: verified — the 5 questions exist in eg-evaluate today, both in the TLDR (compressed comma-separated list) and as ... opening clauses of paragraphs in the body. No question 6 was invented.
load_bearing_content_audit.link_target_demonstrative_value_audit: Each of the 5 link destinations is the most directly demonstrative section in the review for the corresponding failure mode. Alternative destinations considered and rejected: question 1 → killshots (focuses on 6 kill-shot binary tests, not the broader non-discrimination pattern); question 5 → 2-2-tidal-pattern-one-spike-vs-two-bulges (excellent example but specific to tidal physics, narrower than 6.6b's structural de-duplication treatment).

kernel_of_truth: The original four-up sc-hero (now demoted by EXP-255) and the round-1 dashboard treatment that the round-2 reviewer praised conveyed something real: the reader needs scannable signals on first viewport before committing to long-form prose. The 5-question preface honors that by being a scannable methodology guide — five short questions, each a clickable shortcut to where the failure mode is demonstrated. It does NOT recreate the dashboard-feel (which the operator deliberately removed), but it gives the reader the same affordance: orient quickly, drill in only where you want to.
why_kernel_doesnt_break_module: The dashboard-feel of the round-1 sc-hero was load-bearing for credibility (per round-1 designer brief), but its specific implementation (four-up stat tiles at hero scale) was not. EXP-255 redistributes those stats across multiple surfaces (verdict bar above, body-row figures within the new thesis-hero, accuracy-math-section below). EXP-257 adds a fifth surface — the methodology preface — that gives the reader navigational orientation without recreating the 'all numbers shouting at once' problem the operator was solving for. The preface is at body weight (font-size 0.95rem) and uses the muted --ink-3 visual treatment, so it sits as a quiet utility not a competing hero element.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adds a fifth structured block to the Overview (verdict bar + falsifiability-module + thesis-hero + evaluate-preface + accuracy-math-section). Is the Overview becoming a wall of widgets?
anticipated_objections.response: Each block has a distinct rhetorical job: (1) verdict bar = 'how the catalog actually lands'; (2) falsifiability-module = 'what would change our verdict'; (3) thesis-hero = the headline claim; (4) evaluate-preface = 'how to think about ANY claim like this'; (5) accuracy-math-section = the arithmetic backing the headline. They progress from concrete tally → narrow self-criticism → headline → universal methodology → arithmetic. The progression is intentional — readers can stop at any depth and have read a coherent argument. The visual treatments are deliberately distinct (verdict bar = horizontal bars; falsifiability-module = grid with status badges; thesis-hero = display serif + figure row; evaluate-preface = numbered Q&A with right-aligned link labels; accuracy-math = two-column prose) so the eye doesn't pattern-match them as 'more of the same'.

anticipated_objections.objection: Question 4 ('Is the claim falsifiable?') links to #misleading, but the canonical 'falsifiability' content is in #eg-falsifiability ('What Would Change Our Verdict'). Why not link there?
anticipated_objections.response: Two reasons. (1) The operator HNOTE specifies each question 'links to the section in the review that demonstrates it' — eg-falsifiability is about OUR review's falsifiability (we list four conditions that would change our verdict), while the question 'Is the claim falsifiable?' applied to the dome model is demonstrated by the bucket of dome WINs that fail this test (Misleading & Unfalsifiable, with 4 explicit Unfalsifiable verdicts). The question is about evaluating the dome's claims, so it should link to the dome-claim bucket. (2) The eg-falsifiability section is already linked from the EXP-256 falsifiability-module's caption, and from the eg-evaluate full preface (linked from the EXP-257 caption). Linking it a third time on the Overview risks redirect collisions in the click-stream.

anticipated_objections.objection: Question 5's link to dedup-analysis (6.6b) requires a tab switch from 'overview' to 'predictions'. Is the cross-tab jump confusing?
anticipated_objections.response: Cross-tab links are standard throughout the site (every onclick="showTab('...');return false" call is a cross-tab jump) and the EXP-255 thesis-hero eclipse badge already does the same (overview → pages tab for #p4-426). The site's tab system handles back-button navigation correctly via the history.replaceState/pushState machinery (lines 1810-1879). Reader expectations are calibrated.

anticipated_objections.objection: The existing eg-evaluate section is a long-form prose treatment with the medicine analogy that round-2 reviewer specifically praised. By creating a Overview preface, are we cannibalizing the section's discoverability?
anticipated_objections.response: No — we are amplifying it. Today, the medicine-analogy treatment lives in the Evaluation Guide tab and a casual reader of the Overview never sees it. With the EXP-257 preface, every Overview reader sees the 5 questions on first load and has a one-click 'Full preface →' caption link to the medicine-analogy treatment. Discoverability of eg-evaluate goes UP, not down. The Overview preface is a TLDR; the section is the long-form.

anticipated_objections.objection: The preface uses '5 questions' but the eg-evaluate section's TLDR also uses the same 5 questions verbatim. Are we duplicating content unnecessarily?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — and that's the point. The HNOTE explicitly directs the analyst to 'distill the existing prose into 5 short questions' and 'place ABOVE the thesis sentence as eyebrow / preface block, OR below the thesis at body weight'. The duplication is between Overview (orientation) and Evaluation Guide tab (deep treatment). This is a hub-and-spoke pattern, not unnecessary duplication. If a future edit changes the questions, both surfaces will need updating — flagged in the integration notes for the decider.

anticipated_objections.objection: The preface reads as a 'how-to-evaluate-any-claim' methodology, but the round-2 reviewer wanted the Overview to put the dome on the line. Doesn't this dilute the dome-specific argument?
anticipated_objections.response: The preface comes BELOW the dome-specific thesis-hero (EXP-255: 'None of the 71 predictions...') and BELOW the falsifiability-module (EXP-256: dome-specific conditions). By the time the reader reaches the preface, they have already received the dome-specific punchline twice. The preface then offers a portable methodology — which serves a different rhetorical function: 'we are not asking you to take our verdict on faith; here are the questions you should be asking, and here is where in the review we test each one.' This is methodological transparency, not dome-specific dilution.

anticipated_objections.objection: EXP-257 changes only build-scripts/generate-html.js, not data/sections.json. The HNOTE Item 4 'Files modified' line says BOTH may be needed. Why not edit sections.json?
anticipated_objections.response: The HNOTE explicitly says 'data/sections.json (if new prose required)'. EXP-257 introduces NO new prose — the 5 questions are reused verbatim from existing content in eg-evaluate, and the caption is two short utility sentences. There is no section-prose change to make in sections.json, so editing it would be needless churn. The decider workflow will commit only generate-html.js for code; the EXP/proposal/tracker/human-notes files are operational. This matches EXP-256's footprint.

anticipated_objections.objection: Build will the live-count substitution work when EXP-255 hasn't integrated yet? The thesis-hero substitution uses ${counts.total}; does EXP-257 introduce any new template variables?
anticipated_objections.response: No new template variables. EXP-257's HTML is purely static — no ${...} substitutions. Build pipeline impact is zero: it renders the same regardless of build context. This was deliberate to keep EXP-257 robust against EXP-255 integration order and against any future renames of counts.total or related variables.

holes_addressed: Operator HNOTE Item 4 — promote 'How to Evaluate Any Scientific Claim' to a 5-question Overview preface, each question a link to the section demonstrating it. Delivered.
Round-2 reviewer NEW callout #1 (How-to-Evaluate promotion) — addressed by surfacing the existing eg-evaluate 5-question framework on the Overview tab with click-through to demonstration sections.
Round-1 reviewer F2 (give the reader a methodology before plunging into 71 WINs) — addressed by placing the 5 questions on the first viewport, immediately below the headline verdict.
decider_instructions: Apply both patch_segments to build-scripts/generate-html.js (HTML insert before the unique anchor `<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">`; CSS insert after the unique anchor `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}`). Each anchor is verified unique with `grep -c` returning 1 and is robust against EXP-255 / EXP-256 integration order. After applying, run `node build.js html` to regenerate docs/index.html, then `node test.js` to confirm no regressions. Suggested curmudgeon routing: this is a substantive Overview rework per HNOTE explicit directive — route through curmudgeon BEFORE auto-integration.
curmudgeon_review_focus: Verify the 5 questions match the canonical eg-evaluate questions verbatim (medicine-analogy treatment intact in Evaluation Guide tab).
Verify each link destination is the strongest demonstrative section for the corresponding failure mode (alternative-destination rejections in cross_references.five_question_link_targets are honest).
Stress-test placement: BELOW thesis-hero vs ABOVE — does the preface dilute the thesis as climax, or does it reinforce 'here's why we got here'?

Stress-test redundancy with EXP-256 falsifiability-module: do the two structured blocks read as complementary (universal methodology vs review-specific verdict criteria) or as competing? Visual differentiation (left-rail color, list style) is the analyst's hedge.
Stress-test whether question 4 ('Is the claim falsifiable?') should link to #misleading or to #eg-falsifiability — the analyst chose #misleading per HNOTE wording but reasonable people could differ.
Stress-test cross-tab link UX: question 5 jumps overview → predictions tab. Does the back-button behavior on the site preserve the reader's place?

Verify no copy regression: that adding a fifth structured Overview block doesn't push critical content (downloads, verdict legend, Part 1 link) below a usability threshold on small screens. Confirm responsive @media(max-width:720px) treatment renders correctly.
Apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric to the new visual treatment.
Verify the EXP-257 patch_segments anchors remain unique after EXP-253/254/255/256 land (anchors are stable today; curmudgeon should re-verify against latest main at review time).

notes_for_decider: EXP-257 completes the EXP-255 / EXP-256 / EXP-257 trio for HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001. With Items 1+2+5 (EXP-255), Item 3 (EXP-256), and Item 4 (EXP-257) all delivered, the HNOTE flips from 'pending' to 'consumed' once the decider integrates all three. Item 2 was delivered as a no-op audit bundled in EXP-255 (the four-up scorecard tiles are demoted, not removed; the verdict bar chart was already canonical). Curmudgeon routing recommended for all three — the trio together represents a substantive Overview rework that warrants adversarial review before integration.
tldr_evidence: The existing eg-evaluate section in the Evaluation Guide tab contains five canonical questions (verbatim in its TLDR and as paragraph headings) for evaluating any scientific claim. None of these questions surfaces on the Overview today, so a casual reader who never scrolls past the Overview tab never sees the methodology framework that the rest of the review applies.
tldr_verdict: EXP-257 inserts a 5-question methodology preface on the Overview, BELOW the thesis-hero (EXP-255) and ABOVE the accuracy-math-section. Each question is a link to the verdict bucket that demonstrates the failure mode it detects (questions 1-4 → 3.4/3.3/3.2/3.6 in tab 'wins'; question 5 → 6.6b in tab 'predictions'). Reuses the existing eg-evaluate 5 questions verbatim — no invented content. Caption links back to the full eg-evaluate medicine-analogy treatment for readers who want the long form.
